title: Handroanthus subtilis
taxonomy: Handroanthus subtilis is a species of tree in the family Bignoniaceae. It is native to South America and is part of the genus Handroanthus, which is commonly known as the Lapacho trees. The genus Handroanthus is further classified under the order Lamiales and the class Magnoliopsida, which are part of the larger kingdom Plantae. Handroanthus subtilis is closely related to other species within the Bignoniaceae family, such as the Handroanthus impetiginosus. The classification of Handroanthus subtilis reflects its evolutionary relationships with other plants and its position within the plant kingdom.

Handroanthus impetiginosus, also known as Lapacho or Tabebuia, is a plant species native to the subtropical regions of South America, belonging to the Bignoniaceae family. The plant is used in traditional medicine to treat various health issues, including fever, pain, and inflammation, due to its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties. The bioactive constituents of Handroanthus impetiginosus include quinones, alkaloids, and flavonoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties.
